locale.label: Language
theme.label: Theme
hero.title: A warm companion, in your terminal
hero.subtitle: A companion for reflection and emotional support, shipped as a Claude Code plugin — evidence-based techniques, plain-Markdown memory under ~/.claudia, nothing uploaded.
install.title: INSTALL
different.title: DESIGN DECISIONS
different.subtitle: Six deliberate choices, each recorded as an ADR in docs/adr/.
different.relationship.title: Relationship first
different.relationship.description: The evidence says the therapeutic bond drives outcomes more than any technique. Claudia's always-on core is relational — empathy, positive regard, alliance — and the modalities are a toolbox she reaches into when indicated.
different.immersion.title: Immersion with a floor
different.immersion.description: Warm and in-character by default — no infantilising disclaimers — but a deterministic safety layer runs on every turn, and a crisis pivot surfaces real human help when danger is detected.
different.natural.title: Natural language first
different.natural.description: Ten slash commands, nothing else — data, safety and orientation controls. Everything therapeutic happens in plain conversation.
different.local.title: Your data, your machine
different.local.description: Memory is plain Markdown under ~/.claudia on your machine. No telemetry, no cloud, nothing uploaded — ever.
different.language.title: Speaks your language
different.language.description: The codebase is English; Claudia converses in your language and writes her deliverables in it. This page comes in English and French, too.
different.continuity.title: Continuity, session to session
different.continuity.description: Claudia opens where you left off — distilled session summaries, goals and themes, never raw transcripts. A working memory you can read and edit.
safety.title: SAFETY FLOOR
safety.body: Claudia is not a licensed clinician, not therapy, and not a medical device. The floor is non-negotiable and wired outside the persona:
safety.point1.title: A deterministic safety layer
safety.point1.description: It runs on every turn, outside the model's discretion — immersion never overrides it.
safety.point2.title: A crisis pivot
safety.point2.description: When danger is detected, Claudia steps out of character and surfaces real human help for your region.
safety.point3.title: /help-now, one command away
safety.point3.description: Crisis and emergency resources for your region, immediately — no conversation required.
privacy.title: ~/.claudia
privacy.body: Everything Claudia remembers is plain Markdown in a local vault. The full contract lives in docs/memory-layout.md — this is the shape:
privacy.tree:
~/.claudia/
├── MEMORY.md            # the index — loaded first
├── person.md            # the distilled person model
├── understanding.md     # working understanding — dated, correctable
├── goals.md             # agreed goals
├── themes.md            # recurring threads
├── themes/              # one note per thread that earned depth
│   └── what steadies me.md
├── people.md            # relationship map — a mermaid ecomap
├── people/              # one fiche per important person
│   └── Devon.md
├── timeline.md          # the life timeline, person-led
├── keepsakes.md         # passages kept verbatim — /keep
├── todo.md              # the shared to-do-later list
├── safety.md            # region resources + standing flags
├── sessions/            # one set of files per session
│   ├── 2026-07-21-9113d5d7.summary.md      # distilled — read on recall
│   └── 2026-07-21-9113d5d7.transcript.md   # verbatim — your archive, not read back
├── dashboard.md         # derived mirror — /dashboard
└── config.json          # your switches — emoji, transcripts, language
privacy.point1: recall opens each session on MEMORY.md + the latest summaries — never transcripts
privacy.point2: distill-session closes it: a distilled summary, stamped frontmatter, plain relative links
privacy.point3: No database, no server, no sync — grep it, diff it, back it up, open it in any editor
privacy.point4: /forget really deletes; /export takes everything with you
commands.title: COMMANDS
commands.subtitle: Ten slash commands total: six data/safety/memory controls, three pull-only orientation aids, one to keep a sentence that landed.
commands.helpNow: Immediately surface crisis resources for your region.
commands.forget: Really delete a memory, a session, or everything.
commands.thread: The thread of the conversation so far — a light reflection to gather back.
commands.dashboard: A bird's-eye mirror of where things are — pulled by you, never recited at you.
commands.keep: Keep a passage that landed, word for word, to re-read whenever.
commands.menu: Not sure where to start? A few things open for you right now.
faq.title: FAQ
faq.q1.question: Is Claudia therapy?
faq.q1.answer: No. Claudia is not a licensed clinician, not therapy, and not a medical device — and she never claims to be. She is a companion for reflection and emotional support. If you are in crisis, /help-now surfaces real human help for your region, and professional care always comes first.
faq.q2.question: What do I need to run her?
faq.q2.answer: Claude Code (the CLI or desktop app) with a Claude subscription. Then two commands: register the marketplace, install the plugin, and start a new session. Claudia runs entirely inside your terminal.
faq.q3.question: Where does my data go?
faq.q3.answer: Claudia's notes never leave your machine — plain Markdown under ~/.claudia that you can read, edit, /export, or /forget, for real. Your conversations run through Claude, like any Claude Code session, under Anthropic's privacy terms. There is no Claudia server.
faq.q4.question: What does she cost?
faq.q4.answer: Claudia is free and MIT-licensed, with the code open on GitHub. You only pay for your own Claude usage, as with any Claude Code session.
faq.q5.question: Which languages does she speak?
faq.q5.answer: Yours. The codebase is English, but Claudia converses in your language and writes her deliverables — exercises, letters, summaries — in it.
cta.readySubtitle: Someone to think alongside, in the terminal you already live in.
legal.body:
Contact email: {contactEmail}.

Hosting provider
Vercel Inc., 440 N Barranca Ave #4133, Covina, CA 91723, United States.

Publication director
Antoine Bernier.


PRIVACY POLICY

Data collected
This site sets no cookies and collects no personal data. Traffic is measured anonymously with Vercel Analytics (cookieless).

Claudia's data
Claudia's memory lives in plain Markdown under ~/.claudia, on your machine — nothing is uploaded to any Claudia server. Conversations are processed by Anthropic's Claude under the terms of your own Claude Code account.

Not a medical device
Claudia is free, open-source software under the MIT license. It is not a medical device, provides no medical or psychological care, and is not a substitute for professional help. In an emergency, contact your local emergency services.
meta.tagline: A warm companion for reflection, in your terminal
